urdu_text:
بیچ چیزیں خود بخود چیزیں نہ شد — بیچ آبن خنجریں تیغیں نہ شد
بیچ حلوائی نہ شد، استاذ کار — تا غلام شکر ریزے نہ شد
مولوی ہرگز نہ شد مولائے روم — تا غلام شمس تبریزے نہ شد
alt_text: Persian/Urdu verses (likely Rumi-tradition couplets in three rows): 'Things did not become things by themselves; iron did not become a blade by itself; / He did not become a master halwai (sweet-maker) until he became a slave to sugar-sprinkling; / He did not become Mawlana of Rumi until he became a slave to Shams of Tabriz.'
notes: Persian-tradition couplets about discipleship — that mastery requires becoming a 'slave' to the master. The third couplet alludes to Mawlana Rumi and his master Shams al-Tabrizi.
